candidates.stmt:
Hi, I am a physicist working as an academic at a local university, where I study materials of the future using computer models. I am also trustee of a local scout group. I have lived in Coventry with my family for over 13 years, having been welcomed into the city’s community. I’m a big fan of the outdoor spaces in Coventry, particularly going for runs and tending to my allotment

I am passionate about active and public transport, road safety and decarbonisation, to make Coventry a healthy, safe and affordable city to move around in. As a trade union member, I also care about precarious employment, and want to ensure that everyone can have a dignified life in Coventry.

I've lived in Coventry since I was born and am passionate about seeing this city develop and improve.

I want to see: 
– Strong labour and working rights in Coventry councils dealings with both public and private sector employers, 
– a larger focus on our local rivers/waterways and the pollution issues they face, and 
– when it comes to our local economy, a focus on small business and social enterprise, rather than multinational corporations.

I’m Tramore (but I go by Tram) and I’ve lived and worked in Coventry for around 8 years now with my Husband and foster son and I work for Coventry University.

I’m very proud of the community I live in and of the diversity which makes Coventry such a wonderful city.

My priorities are will be to ensure that local issues are dealt with promptly and residents concerns are not only heard, but actioned. I’ve seen this spirit from Green Party councillors in Coventry: they’re a visible and constant presence all year round, not just during election time. This type of community engagement is vital to improving our areas.

I am a lifelong local resident with a passion for community and inclusivity. I believe that politics should work for everyone, at all levels. As a public servant, I work hard to deliver meaningful and positive impact to every day people’s lives. I spend my free time enjoying our local green spaces, bird watching and supporting Coventry City FC.

I’ve lived in the Binley and Willenhall ward my whole life and I have always been proud of it’s people and our green spaces. I love to support our local sports teams and am a season ticket holder at the CBS where you can regularly find me enjoying a song and a beer. Play up Sky Blues!

I’m a local mum of three and have worked in education and family support for over 15 years. I now run a tuition and support service, helping neurodivergent children who struggle to thrive in traditional school settings, and I’m also the co-director of a CIC bringing creative, hands-on learning projects to life.

I believe that strong communities are built when people feel supported, connected and hopeful about the future. Living and working in Binley & Willenhall, I see both the strengths of this community and the challenges people face. I want every family here to feel connected and heard, with access to the services and safe spaces they need to flourish.

I’m also passionate about protecting the green spaces that make our local area such a special place to live. Access to nature and clean, safe parks are essential for our children’s wellbeing and for the health of our whole community. These spaces must be nurtured and protected for future generations to come

I want to stand in Binley & Willenhall because I was born and raised in this area, I care about the community and want to see it properly represented.

I work full-time and understand the everyday pressures people are facing, from the cost of living to the condition of our local area.

I’m not a career politician, I’m someone who wants to listen, be visible, and focus on the issues that matter most to residents. I believe in being open, accountable, and working towards a fairer, greener future for our community, while making sure local concerns are heard and acted on.

Paras Jain is standing for the first time in the local council elections, bringing with him a fresh perspective and a strong commitment to the community.

Having lived in Willenhall for over 15 years, Paras understands the area, its people, and the challenges residents face every day. As an engineer by profession, he believes in practical solutions, problem-solving, and getting things done efficiently.

Outside of work, Paras is a DIY enthusiast and an active member of the local community. Whether it’s helping with community initiatives or supporting local activities, he believes in contributing and staying connected.

After seeing the declining condition of the city and particularly the local ward, Paras has decided to step forward and make a difference. He is standing as a Conservative candidate to bring:

1. Better local accountability
2. Focus on clean, safe neighbourhoods
3. Practical improvements that residents can actually see and feel
4. Improved local children play areas

Paras believes Willenhall deserves strong local representation, honest leadership, and real action—not just promises.

I grew up in the East Midlands but I’ve lived in Coventry since 2019. I am proudly Neurodivergent and have worked both within Coventry, Warwickshire and Worcestershire Mind and the NHS since moving to Coventry.

I can see how previous national governments are failing Neurodivergent adults, children and their families and how few supports there are for families locally. I’d like to work to see what the council can do to help support these families.

Coventry has some great green spaces and some incredible wildlife. I never dreamt when I moved here that I would see birds of prey, herons and even deer. These need to be protected so that future generations can experience them. Our rivers need cleaning to encourage an ecosystem that is as wonderfully diverse as the city’s human population is.

I grew up in the West Midlands and I moved to Coventry in 2024. Coventry’s people are warm, energetic and creative, both in their own interests and their care for one another. I want to do my bit to help the city flourish.

I have worked across care services, education and retail and studied sociology and social policy. I believe that it is vital that we ensure our public services work for everyone regardless of their age or background.

I am committed to Coventry Green Party’s initiative to prioritise our libraries, parks and cultural spaces and ensure they are well-funded, vibrant and inclusive. I also advocate for our public transport to be accessible, efficient and safe, better connecting the city whilst prioritising sustainability and reducing environmental harm.

My name is Seb and I work as an academic researcher in sociology – which means I try to understand how society works, why it changes and how government policy affects people’s lives. I have a PhD from University of Warwick, which is what brought me to Coventry twelve years ago.

One of my favourite things about Coventry is the community spirit – I’ve had to move house a few times and always found friendly neighbours who are looking out for me.

If elected as local councillor, my priorities will be to push for an increase in local government funding to tackle the current under-funding crisis and make Coventry a more affordable and environmentally friendly place to live.

I’m a Cov kid and grew up in Eastern Green before embarking on a primary school teaching career that lasted until I retired 4 years ago. I’m also a children’s author and a director of Chugg Productions Ltd, a company formed to make my books into a TV series.

Being a mother and grandmother, like many other women, I’ve become increasingly concerned for our children’s future. Having recently moved back to Coventry after living on a narrowboat for 14 years I’ve become involved with the Green Party and passionately believe in their message of hope. I want to ensure that this message is represented and acted upon in my home city.

I’ve lived in Earlsdon with my family for nearly 20 years.  I was born in Coventry and have lived most of my life here and in Warwickshire.  Coventry is a city of peace and reconciliation, a place of hope and rebirth.

I have worked for the NHS for over 21 years.  I have supported service improvement.  I serve as a church warden at St Barbara’s.  I am passionate about good public services. I’m taking voluntary redundancy, so if elected I would dedicate myself to this.

Politics needs positive change which the Greens can deliver. I want to work with everyone to build a better city for all.  We need unity not division.   We need to campaign for better central funding for education and care to build strong communities.

We need to listen, plan together and build sustainable and safe communities.

I was born and raised in Coventry and have had a long career in music and music education. During the eighties I had some chart success with Cov band ‘King’ and spent most of the 90’s running the Depot Studios audio dept for Coventry City Council. I then ran Warwickshire County Council’s Contemporary and Popular music department for ten years. Having lived on a narrowboat for 14 years I have now retired.

I truly believe that principles of compassion, hope and empathy for our environment and everyone in it provide a sound basis for action, and that that action should stand in opposition to those who seek to divide and undermine our communities.

Born and raised in Coventry, and proud to have called Earlsdon home for more than 18 years. I care deeply about the future of our neighbourhood and Earlsdon Ward deserves a strong, consistent voice that stands up for residents and local businesses, especially at a time when frontline services are under pressure and every pound of public money must deliver genuine value.
I believe in bringing people together, not allowing anyone to divide our community or set neighbour against neighbour. Earlsdon thrives when we look out for one another, support local enterprise, and insist on fairness, transparency, and accountability from those responsible for delivering essential services.
Everything we do should build a just and fair society that rewards hard work, protects the vulnerable, and strengthens the places we call home. Earlsdon will always be a community that leads by example, and I’m committed to helping ensure it continues to do so.

I am a dedicated community-focused individual with a passion for fairness, inclusion, and sustainable development. I believe in practical solutions that improve everyday life for local residents.
I have been part of the Coventry community for several years. I value its diversity, strong community spirit, and rich history, which make it a unique and welcoming place to live and grow.
My priorities are improving local services, supporting small businesses, tackling the cost-of-living crisis, and promoting cleaner, greener neighbourhoods. I will work to ensure every voice is heard and no community is left behind.

I’m Sujata Misala, a passionate community member dedicated to supporting local people and creating positive change. I believe in fairness, inclusion, and building opportunities for everyone.
I have lived in Coventry for several years. It’s a diverse and welcoming city where communities come together. I appreciate its strong sense of unity, culture, and shared support.
My priorities are improving housing, supporting small businesses, and keeping our neighbourhoods clean and safe. I want to listen to residents, strengthen local services, and ensure equal opportunities for all.

Coventry born, Coventry educated. Specialist in Russian and American Politics and Business. 42 years experience in international business, utilities, IT, marketing and deal making.

A little about me.

I was born in Walsgrave hospital and raised in Coventry then Balsall Common where I attended Solihull School before Mid-Warwickshire College for an Art Foundation Merit, trying to be an Art Teacher.

I ended up spending most of my life working in international trade after graduating with a General Studies degree majoring in Business Studies, Russian and American politics from Coventry University (with honours).

I was taught politics and ethics by my headmaster Alan Lee, who now has a monumental building named after him in Solihull School.

Standing to replace Starmer's decrepit Labour Party as Reform are replacing Sunak's dilapidated Tories.

I am a resident of Henley Green after leaving Birmingham in 2008. I have brought up 3 children in Coventry, 2 have autism. 
I have been a volunteer at The Enterprise Club for Disabled People for a little over 6 years. 
What I lack in political knowledge I make up for in passion for my neighborhood. I carry out regular litter picks, have over 300 reports on Fixmystreet ranging from fly tipping, pot holes, broken street lighting, bad parking and vandalism. 
I would like to see harsher penalties for people that park on pavements, stricter regulations around the sale of nitrous oxide/cream chargers, less of our larger homes converted into HMO's and left as family homes, less barber shops/vape shops and nail bars, less student accommodation being built and our green space left alone. 
I just want a nicer city

This election matters as following recent boundary changes this will be an all-out election, meaning you will have three votes this year.

Please be sure to use all three.

We firmly believe local politics is first and foremost about local people and local communities.

Over the many years that we have served our community, we have met many of you and have always made great efforts to resolve the issues that you raise.

Our top priorities to you:
- Protect and improve our local green spaces, parks and play areas
- Prioritise fixing roads and potholes
- Investing in our elderly care homes to protect our most vulnerable
- Recruiting more neighbourhood policing, with bobbies back on the beat
- Invest in our NHS by supporting our nurses and care workers

We care passionately about our community and will continue to work hard to improve local services for Henley Green, Potters Green, Walsgrave and Wood End, we know there is still more to do and are fully aware of the local issues and challenges facing our communities.

Our ask from you is for the opportunity to serve you and our community.

You have THREE votes on May 7th. Please use all 3 votes for your Labour team - Patricia Seaman, Ed Ruane and Neil Rider so that we can continue to work hard for you in Henley.

Don't risk chaos and division - vote for your hard working Henley Labour Team.

Published and promoted by Neil Rider on behalf of Patricia Seaman, Ed Ruane and Neil Rider all of 90
short St Coventry CV1 2LW

I am 61 years old, autistic and love living in Coventry. I have previously stood for the Green Party when living in Solihull and before that Richmond-upon-Thames.

I have previously fallen on hard times and experienced homelessness and the need to use food banks following a relationship breakdown and single parenthood. I am thus very committed to social justice and support for those people who have experienced adversity through no fault of their own.

I dislike wealth inequality and believe Coventry is a place where everyone can feel safe, empowered and important.

I am proud to be Coventry’s first Green councillor. Before that I worked in a range of jobs – from banking to charities. I am a committed Christian and a big 'Doctor Who' fan. I came to Coventry as a student in the late 90s and chose to stay because I love the city’s diversity, history, and culture. Basically I love Coventry's people.

If re-elected I would continue to push to make it easier to get around the city without a car, to protect and improve our green spaces, to reduce our carbon emissions and dependence on expensive fossil fuels, and to help our poorest and most disadvantaged families improve their lives.

I have always been involved in community projects and helping people with advice and support, through various jobs and volunteering including for over a decade now helping to run a community farm just outside the city. Three years ago I was elected as a councillor for Holbrooks and have been working in the ward, supporting individuals and community groups as well as learning how the Council works on a policy making level.

I have lived in Coventry all my adult life. I came here to go to University, fell in love with the city and my husband so we made our home here. I am inspired by the history of Coventry, how we have re-invented ourselves over and over again, always bringing in new people and ideas and I believe we can do that again to make a better future.

My priority, if re-elected, is to improve communications, so the people of Coventry have a real say in what happens, and to support local projects which are aiming to improve the city by working together.

Since being elected, I’ve been asking for a city-wide plan for walking, wheeling and cycling so that various cycle routes actually connect up and make it easier to get around the city, so I will continue to work on that. I also want to push for more green spaces and community growing projects in the city.

I am, by nature, someone who just wants to make the world a better place. I have always voted on what I think is best for my community. As a councillor I would bring that philosophy to my work and look at how I can continue to help make Coventry and Holbrooks a better place.

I first came to Coventry as a student in the early 90’s and settled here. I raised my family here and value its history, diversity and strong community. I have worked in a wide range of jobs including IT, Finance before becoming a Secondary School Teacher.

As a councillor I want to; ensure Holbrook’s residents feel heard and see real responses to their concerns, Campaign for better funding for schools to ensure they are well staffed and well maintained and provide better support for students who struggle to engage with education of all ages.

I have been an activist for the last 30 years representing people in my workplace, and the wider community.

Having been born and spent my entire life in Coventry I have seen changes both for the good and some not so good. I believe Coventry people deserve to be represented by one of their own, someone that understands the challenges we are all facing during this cost of living crisis.

Coventry not only faces uncertainty regarding our relationship with Europe but the devastating prospect that job losses have had on our communities we need a strong voice for Coventry. I hope like me you feel Coventry belongs to its people and you will consider voting for me in this election.

I am a mother of 3 and a teacher. I am passionate about education and constructing a better world for future generations. I was born and raised in Spain and moved to the UK to become a secondary school teacher. I have lived in Coventry for 2 years and I love the diversity of the people here. I enjoy going to the market on a Saturday, reading, hiking with my family and gardening.

If elected I feel Coventry has more possibilities for cycling and walking, the city could benefit from having less cars on their streets. Improvements to public transport are necessary to achieve this. I would start by promoting educational workshops in primary schools about environmental issues. I do believe people would be happier in Coventry if the streets were clean and there was less pollution.

I am Kolapo Abubakar, an experienced Specification Technologist with over 15 years in the food industry, a University of Salford alumnus, and a proud resident of Longford, Coventry.

As a Green Party candidate for Longford Ward Councillor, I am committed to honest representation and practical leadership that reflects residents’ needs.

My priorities are cleaner streets, safer neighbourhoods, affordable housing, better local services, and strong support for green and sustainable initiatives, working closely with residents and community groups to make Longford a better and more inclusive place to live and work.

I joined the Green Party in 2015 from a Lib-Dem family background, convinced that politics has to be radically different for our planet and for the next generation.

I moved from Yorkshire when the charity I work for moved its headquarters here, and I love the vibrancy and diversity of this city, with its rich history and entrepreneurial spirit.

My priorities will be for you, your family and your neighbours to be able to live and work safely and securely, without prejudice, near good schools, reliable public transport, well-funded health and social services, in pleasant and attractive surroundings, where food can be locally grown, while our power supplies, transport and other resources don't cost the earth.

I am an expert in project management & training. I have a passion for networking, and I look for challenges. I have worked in Europe, Asia, Africa, and North/South America.

I exploit my know-how, expertise, and professional network to help individuals and institutions achieve and thrive even in challenging circumstances. Currently, I am an Associate Professor in Project Management at Northeastern University London, while I previously was the Course Director for the Project Management MSc at Coventry University Business School, and the Course Director for the Engineering Project Management MSc at Coventry University Engineering, Environment and Computing Faculty. I am PMP, PRINCE2, CDPM, 6Sigma, SCRUM, and AgilePM certified, but also a chartered manager (CMgr) & engineer (CEng) with 30+ years of experience in project management, operation, quality assurance, and supply chain management.

KEY PROMISES
Bring back Stoke Green Fair days for all ages
Bag it, bin it — stronger enforcement, cleaner streets
Creative skills for young people — learning from local talent.
Fix roads, pavements, and parks for all residents.
Support local shops — reduce costs and protect them.

Clean Streets & Green Spaces

 Tackle dog fouling and litter hotspots
 Faster fly-tipping removal
 Cleaner parks and safer play areas

Safer Streets & Transport
 Improved street lighting
 Safer roads and pavements
 Safer routes to school for children

Children, Young People and Families
 More safe spaces and activities for young people, including creative workshops.
 Support youth services and Aspire community hubs.
 Promote walk-to-school wardens for safer routes.

Backing Local Business
 Push for lower operating costs
 Support retrofitting & energy efficiency
 Protect the character of our high street

Born and raised in Coventry, I am deeply committed to the community that shaped me. My academic background in Sociology, combined with extensive volunteer work, fuels my passion for fostering togetherness and resilience.

Inspired by Coventry’s history of reconciliation and it’s vibrant, diverse culture, I am standing for council with three core priorities: education, health, and employment.

I advocate for expanded adult learning, better resources for schools and early years, and addressing health inequalities through better outreach, improved housing, and green space accessibility. My vision involves a green industrial transition, supporting social enterprises and sustainable jobs to ensure a fairer future.

I aim to translate Green Party values into pragmatic local action, ensuring Coventry becomes a model of inclusive sustainability for every resident.

I have lived in Coventry since 1998 and joined the Green Party in 2014.

As a parent, I am passionate about education, ensuring that children have access to green spaces, clean air to breathe and a safe environment to grow up in.

If I’m successful in being elected as a councillor, I will aim to be a voice of reason, representing the concerns of Coventry families with an eye on the future city our children will inherit.

I'm a software developer, working remotely ever since Covid. I keep guinea pigs, and I spend my free time doing lighting for amateur theatre, or attending LARP (live action roleplaying) events.
I came to Coventry for university more than twenty years ago, and liked it well enough to stick around! I like that although it's a city, it feels small and vibrant, with an accessible city centre.
My main priorities will be related to disabled access and social care. Services are stretched way too thin. Physical access needs championing with all the construction going on. Youth services have been gutted nationally in the last few decades — we can't complain the kids are spending all their time online if there's nothing else for them to do!

I am a queer, hardworking, nature-loving creative. Currently working in the art and culture sector, I create experiences that celebrate Coventry and enrich peoples’ lives.

Since coming to Coventry to study in 2015, I have discovered I feel at home with the down-to-earth nature of its people. The diversity of its communities and its cultures have made it a place I am proud to call my home.

If elected, my priorities would be: to grow and protect our natural and green spaces; advocate for the welfare of marginalised and under-represented communities; ensure transparency and accountability in the city’s decision-making.

I believe Coventry deserves a greener, fairer and more resilient future — both socially
and environmentally. I will champion transparent governance and community-led priorities, and working tirelessly to create a Coventry that is sustainable, inclusive and prosperous for all.

For the past 15 years I've worked in Coventry and across the West Midlands within the renewable energy sector, helping businesses switch to commercial solar solutions, reduce carbon emissions and lower energy costs as part of the wider transition to net-zero.

My experience reflects my commitment to real, practical climate action. This can benefit the people of Coventry by lowering bills, creating skilled local jobs, and improving the quality of life in our communities.

I have been a resident of Coventry since 1997 and a member of the Green Party since 2014.
I’m a non-driver and, if elected, would work to improve public transport links, alongside cycle and pedestrian routes within the city of Coventry. I will also aim to improve Coventry’s green spaces for the benefit of both wildlife and people. I am also keen to promote Coventry’s historic and cultural heritage.

Coventry born and raised technology enthusiast and nature lover with roots in our city’s industrial history. From my first job at the Broadgate Primark to IT management in Coventry’s automotive sector today, I’ve seen the power of efficiency.

As your councillor, I’ll focus on the basics: fixing potholes, tackling litter, and ensuring safe housing. I’ll use my career experience to boost council interactions and help St Michael’s residents access money saving technology and digital skills. I promise to listen and work with you to remove the barriers to our potential and restore Coventry’s pioneering spirit.

I love politics and organising, and trying to make a real difference. I’m a socialist. I believe we can build a better world by meeting people’s needs and sharing more fairly. I like heavy metal, my dog, and my allotment.

I grew up in Coventry and love its unpretentiousness and multiculturalism. In St Michael’s, you see people doing amazing things with very little – and some of the best, most affordable food anywhere!

My priority is making Coventry fairer and more liveable: warmer homes, lower bills, better transport, and stronger community support. I want to back local projects, invest in green spaces, and make sure people have a real say in decisions.

I am a disabled, black, gay refugee who has been homeless twice. I understand poverty, discrimination and the effects of failed policy. I have dedicated the last decade to working for charities and want the opportunity to help more people in bigger ways.

I have lived in Coventry for 13 years. Our multi-cultural city is a shining example of how acceptance and tolerance make the world a better place. Our people are kind, hardworking, and resilient, and we look out for each other.

I want to make our neighbourhoods safer and homes more livable. While also supporting local businesses. This involves providing equal access to resources and opportunity and supporting those amongst us who need a bit more help to thrive in society. I also want to protect our children from the influence of gangs and crime.
Bradley Robertson is an autistic man who has lived in Coventry since 2008. He attended Finham Park Secondary School. He is a volunteer at The Enterprise Club for Disabled People in Upper Stoke.

Will you be glad to see the back of the Tories – but have no enthusiasm for Starmer’s Labour?  Feel like we're paying more for things that don’t work properly, yet the rich are getting richer all the time?  Feel let down by all the main parties on Gaza, on the Post Office and infected blood scandals, on housing, on the NHS and local services?  We agree. Starmer's Labour will be the next government, but that won't be the change we need! In Coventry East, we have a chance to tell them what we think by sending a socialist working-class fighter who will fight for the issues that Labour has dropped and help build the pressure outside Parliament for a new party that will really represent us.

"When all the establishment parties are the same, insisting ordinary working people pay for the crisis in their system, you need a strong, independent socialist to fight for you and your family. I’ve proven I can do that job on the council and in parliament - help me be your voice again when the next government lets us down like the last.” Dave Nellist, June 2024
Alysha is one of three Green Party candidates for the new Tile Hill & Canley ward of Coventry in the local election on 7 May 2026.

I moved to Tile Hill at the age of 16 to study at Hereward College. I have remained living in Tile Hill for over 22 years.

Whilst achieving a Masters at Coventry University, I was a student union representative and a trustee of a local charity, helping to improve the lives of local people.

Having a disability means I understand what it is like to feel that things aren't working for you. It gives me the skills to find solutions to problems that will fix them fully if elected.

Through my community work and training to be a teacher. I will bring hope, fairness and opportunities to you, the people of Coventry, focusing on the things that matter to you.

My top 3 priorities are:
– Fixing pavements and potholes 
– Tackling anti social behaviour 
– Promoting and supporting local businesses and services.

A Canadian by birth and upbringing, my family and I moved up to Coventry from London nearly 20 years ago and I’ve worked as a free-lance performing arts teacher and performance maker as well as working with the Belgrade Theatre access department, providing Audio Description for patrons living with a visual impairment.

Despite not being interested in politics for most of my life, I joined the green party a few years ago when I realised that people and businesses couldn’t lead the way in the fight to save the environment, and that it is a government’s fundamental responsibility to do so.

I like to keep fit by doing yoga or walking my two dogs but I am most passionate about creative expression in the community and keeping the hopes and dreams of the Coventry City of Culture 2021 alive for the next 10 years at least.

Politics is broken! Some councillors in the city have been elected longer than i have been alive, and what has changed in that time… politics should be about people and not false promises.

Having been elected in 2024 with a majority of over 500 votes, I know im best placed to contest this election on behalf of residents in Tile Hill and Canley.

We need
- more council, social and affordable housing. Housing is a human right
- more opportunities for our young people from more Youth Centres, free school meals and free childcare
- better protected green spaces and mitigation against climate change
- accessible public transport

I came to Coventry in 2010 to study at the university and found a place to call home, which I now share with my husband and two young children. I enjoy the down to earth people and the diverse cultures represented throughout the cities communities, history and food.

For the last 10 years I have worked as a Project Manger and I feel this experience will allow me to push for change if I am elected as a councilor. There are many great things about this city but we need to keep striving for a greener, cleaner future that supports residents and businesses to invest in Green initiatives.

The whole city can prosper when pollution is reduced, when communities and living conditions are safe, and when wildlife is supported so it can thrive.
Edward is standing as a Green Party candidate for the Upper Stoke ward in the Coventry City local election on 7 May 2026.

I was born and raised in Coventry and am a mother and grandmother of Coventry kids. After working in youth and community work all of my working life I am now retired and living in Hillfields.

I love Coventry because it is a diverse and vibrant city with plenty going on, full of interesting people, surrounded by beautiful countryside and with loads of potential.

For Coventry people to have good lives we all need economic security; good education and activities for our children; and friendly, safe and clean neighbourhoods. Councils cannot provide all of that, but I will work hard to ensure Coventry City Council always puts these needs first when making decisions and spending money.

I am a student at the University of Warwick and I am proud to call Coventry my home. Having lived variously within the Wainbody ward and just outside its boundaries over the last few years, I have developed a good understanding of the area and how it serves everyone who lives here - from all walks of life.

Students form an integral part of Coventry's and Wainbody's population. I will push to ensure that the needs of students and other residents work hand-in-hand, not against each other.

If elected, I will push for improvements to bus routes across the ward, hold developers to account to ensure their developments are up to scratch, and help to ensure public amenities are properly maintained.

I am not a Coventry native (I’m originally from Scotland) but arriving in the city in the late eighties to work at Massey Ferguson, I found a very welcoming city with many positive qualities. Wainbody Ward embodies many of those qualities, with its many attractive green spaces, thriving businesses, schools, sports clubs and much more besides.

Coventry has proved to be a great place to live but it is not without its problems, some of which I have learnt about when door knocking.

Our current councillors are doing a good job addressing many of these issues but there is always more to do, and should I be successfully elected, I would be happy to use my personal experiences and qualities to work towards delivering improvements in Coventry.

My background is in Science, Engineering and IT, and I believe my personal skillset would very much complement the skillsets of councillors I have met while campaigning for Labour.

I have a personal interest in sports (and my own Rugby Club is within Wainbody's ward boundaries), recreation and outdoor spaces, but I pledge to engage fully with the Wainbody community and work with local groups and residents via social media, regular door knocking, stalls and meetings. Thereby ensuring that no one is left behind.

I am a local builder who lives and works in Coventry, carrying out repairs and refurbishments predominantly in private homes. When not at work I enjoy being a member of the Warwickshire Wildlife Trust, and visiting their sites in and around the city and further afield.

I have lived in Coventry for about five years, and appreciate it's emphasis on moving away from traffic pollution, with such things as cycle lanes, electric buses and kerbside electric car charging.

If elected I should like to continue the push in this direction. I see being a councillor as an opportunity to give back to the city, and would concentrate on serving the residents of Wainbody ward and assisting them with their issues to the best of my ability.

I live in Coventry with my family, and was a soldier for many years in the British Army. 

Currently a paralegal, I have used my legal knowledge to help many people in Coventry and beyond, 

Britain is broken, and the threat to Britain is at an all time high, As an ex-soldier and volunteer for Coventry Citizens Advice Bureau, I have seen first hand the scale of the issues, and helped to mitigate those problems.

Day in day out, I come across cases not only unique to Coventry, but repeated similarly up and down the country.

I am standing for Reform UK because we are a realistic and listening political party, with the answers to solve the many very British problems that we face.  

I will be a powerful voice for Coventry East.

I have lived in Coventry since 1991 when I moved here to train as an Occupational Therapist and never left. I have worked in the National Health Service since qualifying in 1994 and currently work with a community team for dementia and older people’s mental health services.

My priorities for Coventry if elected would be to maintain and improve services for people living with dementia and their carers and vulnerable and disabled people more broadly. I would also advocate for the council to take more action on making our city more sustainable for the future and would stand up to preserve green spaces for people and wildlife alike.

I live and work in Coventry and I’m standing in this election because I love Coventry and I want to do what I can for it to be a happy, healthy and successful city. For over forty years I have worked with local communities to support people.

Everyone deserves a decent home, good quality education and a health service they can rely on when they need it. Nobody, young or old, should feel excluded, and everybody should be able to enjoy a clean, green city – not struggling to make ends meet.

In my working life, I have helped set up lots of projects for families, young people and older people to increase their quality of life. I also have a detailed knowledge of how local councils function and I would use this knowledge and experience to get the best possible results for the people of Coventry.

Recently retired after a long and very enjoyable career in local schools, I am now looking for new ways to help and support our communities.  
I originally came to Coventry in the late 1970s to study and have lived and/or worked here ever since. My children and grandchildren are Coventry kids, and I’m very proud of my adopted city.
I’m standing for election because I want to ensure that Coventry continues to be a kind, safe city for everyone who is lucky enough to find their way here, and to help build a fairer, more sustainable future for us all.

The last year has been tough. Having contracted Covid-19, along with my wife, last year I know from personal experience what a nasty disease it is. The return to normality has not, and will not, be quick. However we move a step closer to that normality with the return of the local elections.

I have represented you at the Council House for the last five years along with Cllr Julia Lepoidevin and Cllr Peter Male. I am standing for re-election because it has been an honour and a privilege to represent Woodlands Ward as your Councillor. I have always worked hard on behalf of the community on the issues that mattered, large or small.

I’ve helped residents with missed bin collections, got pot holes repaired, supported and organised litter picks and the Speedwatch initiative. At the time of writing we are also very close to a new defibrillator coming on stream in Sutherland Avenue. There have been bigger campaigns too like the fight to secure the return of education at the site of the Woodlands Academy and to secure our public spaces against travellers. I’ve also worked with the Mayor of the West Midlands Mayor Andy Street to save the greenbelt.

I have a strong record of action and I promise much more.
My determination to work hard for the people of Woodlands Ward has not diminished, if anything it burns brighter than it did five years ago. So once again I’m asking for your support so I can continue to be accessible and campaign for what matters to you.

I've lived in Coventry for three years, and have met such a diverse community here. On a good day, the city centre is lovelier than anywhere in London – people are far more friendly too! I'm a student at Warwick University, studying History.

Like many, I was a Labour supporter who left when it became clear they stood only for power, and I'm proud to be part of Zack Polanski's movement for real change.

All our councils are being let down by the central government, but also by complacent councillors happy to serve it. Change starts at this grassroots level, politics that serves people, not parties or opaque lobbies.

Electing people willing to challenge the narrative of forced decline is how we begin to turn things around.

I am proud to stand as a candidate for the Green Party of England and wales at the upcoming local elections. I’m a creative professional and long-time local resident, committed to strengthening community wellbeing, protecting green spaces and ensuring transparent, accountable decision making at a council level.

I believe local government works best when it listens carefully, acts with integrity and puts people and the environment at the heart of every policy. I am standing to help build a fairer, healthier and more sustainable future for our community.

I am a nearly retired IT System Test Analyst who likes to travel in the most ecofriendly way possible and I love colour.

I have lived in Coventry since 1983. I came for family reasons and stayed for the good transport links, the history, green spaces and the diverse people. I have been running a local weekly children’s group for over 30 years.

My priorities for Coventry are to improve Public Transport, by increasing frequency and adding routes but keep costs down. This will ultimately cut down on pollution and our reliance on fossil fuels. I want to preserve our green spaces, which are often full of wildlife. We need to protect and promote them, to let local people know all the different activities they can sustainably carry out in our green spaces, to help them care for and be proud of them.

A Fresh Start for Coventry
Over the past six years, through my own experiences dealing with the council, I have seen first-hand where things are going wrong. Too often, residents are met with poor communication, a lack of empathy, and decisions that don’t reflect the needs of the people.
This council has been in power for too long. It’s time for fresh thinking, new energy, and leadership that truly cares about our city and its people.
I am standing to bring honesty, accountability, and a genuine desire to help.

Cleaner, Safer Streets
Coventry should be a city we are proud to call home.
Regular street cleaning and faster action on fly-tipping 
Proper, long-lasting pothole repairs 
Stronger action on antisocial behaviour 
Working with police and communities to reduce crime

A City Centre to Be Proud Of
Our city centre should be welcoming, vibrant, and thriving.
Cleaner, safer public spaces 
Support for good-quality shops and local businesses 
Pause further parking reductions until a reliable transport system is fully in place 
Create a city centre people want to visit and spend time in

Transport That Works for Everyone
We all support greener travel—but it must be practical and safe.
Develop a joined-up, reliable transport network before removing parking 
Make electric buses dependable, accessible, and affordable 
Plan roads and cycleways with safety for everyone in mind—not placing cycle lanes directly in front of driveways or creating hazards 
Work with residents to find safer, better-designed routes for cyclists and drivers alike 
Listen to residents before making major transport decisions

Fairness in Housing and Education
Everyone deserves a fair chance in Coventry.
Greater transparency and fairness in housing 
Support better outcomes and opportunities in education 
Put residents first in decision-making

Jobs, Opportunity, and a Future for Young People
We must give young people a reason to stay.
Support businesses that create real, long-term jobs 
Encourage inv

I moved to Coventry with my wife and three children almost two years ago after several years working and living in Germany. I grew up in Derby so feel at home here in the Midlands.

I am an associate professor of bioinformatics at Coventry University, researching the evolutionary origins of lifespan variation and healthy ageing in animals.

When not working, I enjoy watching my son’s football team, and I am becoming a fan of the Sky Blues. My family and I are nature lovers and passionate about securing a safer, fairer and greener future for our children.

Kirthika is a forensic science graduate who after working in the field for a few years, made the leap to healthcare in order to have a more meaningful impact on people’s lives.

She joined the Green party for the same reason, wanting to uplift those in her community and create a better life for everyone.

In her spare time she can be found reading high fantasy books and working on her current crochet project.
